headline: Fading Curtains & Bright Sunshine
subtext: Ratepayers are expected to grin and bear the discomfort of sun-baked houses with faded curtains. The local Council seems more concerned about filling potholes than repairing windows.
body:
Wollongong's ever-vigilant Council is cracking down on the city's most pressing issue - fading curtains due to excessive sunlight exposure.

In a sudden move that has taken many locals by surprise, Wollongong City Council has declared war on one of Australia's favourite pastimes: soaking up the sun. In a bid to protect our beloved drapes from the harsh rays of the sun, The Council Chambers have decreed a total ban on outdoor leisure activities during daylight hours.

"Councillor Cr1, who asked not to be named, explained that 'the fading of our curtains is an issue that cannot be ignored any longer. We must take action before all our curtains are reduced to mere translucent remnants.'"

This move has been met with mixed reactions from the residents. While some see it as a necessary measure to protect their precious drapes, others believe this decision is the Council's way of controlling their daily lives.

Local Ratepayer John Smith had this to say: "I think this is just another ploy by our so-called benevolent council to control us. What next? A ban on washing our clothes outdoors?"

Wollongong's streets have become eerily quiet as residents abide by the new regulation, which prevents any outdoor activities in daylight hours. The once-bustling Corso has turned into a veritable ghost town.

Even The Steyne, known for its vibrant nightlife, stands empty and abandoned during the day. Locals are now forced to enjoy their favourite pastimes - surfing, swimming or sunbathing - only during the twilight hours.

As for B-Line patrons, they now have an abundance of time to catch up on their reading while waiting for a bus that never seems to come.

It remains to be seen how effective this new regulation will be in saving our curtains from fading away. What is certain though is that residents are not too happy about losing their daily dose of sunshine.

professor.title: From The Archives
professor.content: The archives are currently closed due to a flea infestation.
